channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Refugee Experiences Highlight Benefits of Settling in Regional Australian Towns
item.description: A recent study, 'Settling Well in Regional Australia', explored the experiences of 628 people from refugee backgrounds living in regional towns such as Albury, Wodonga, Mildura, Nhill, Orange, Cowra, ...

item.title: Traffic Affected by Flooding at Molo River Along Eldama Ravine-Molo Road
item.description: Traffic along the Eldama Ravine-Maji Mazuri-Molo Road has been seriously affected due to flooding at the Molo River. According to the Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA), heavy rains upstream ca...

item.title: Charlie Ang petitions for removal from Manila kidnapping case
item.description: The legal team of fugitive businessman Charlie “Atong” Ang has formally requested to be excluded from the ongoing Manila court case involving the disappearance of cockfight enthusiasts. Ang’s lawyer, ...

item.title: Islamabad High Court Cancels Two-Strikes Rule for Civil Service Promotions
item.description: The Islamabad High Court has struck down the 'two-strikes' rule that barred senior bureaucrats from promotion to BS-22 after being passed over twice. Justice Raja Inaam Ameen Minhas ruled that the Rec...

item.title: EU and Australia sign free trade deal and establish defence partnership
item.description: On March 24, 2026, the European Union and Australia signed the final text of a free trade agreement at the Australian Parliament House, marking a significant milestone in their economic and strategic ...

item.title: Zanzibar launches digital system to protect forests from illegal logging
item.description: The Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar has introduced a new digital technology aimed at monitoring and controlling illegal logging, particularly in sensitive mangrove and upland forest areas. The la...

item.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on southern Beirut as tensions with Hezbollah intensify; Trump claims progress in Iran talks
item.description: On March 24, 2026, Israel carried out a fresh series of air strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut, a stronghold of Hezbollah, in one of the latest escalations in the Iran-Israel conflict that has ...

item.title: Six female students harassed during Ozoro festival, no rape reported — SUG president
item.description: During the recent Aluedo festival at Uruamudhu quarters in Ozoro kingdom, six female students of Southern Delta University claimed they were sexually harassed by some hoodlums, but no rape occurred. T...

item.title: Gold Rally in 2026 Supports Rand; Traders Monitor Key USDZAR Levels
item.description: In 2026, gold has been one of the standout performers, rising roughly 22%, which has had a noticeable effect on the USDZAR exchange rate. South African traders observe that when gold strengthens, the ...

item.title: Trainspotting Musical Set for London's West End with Multimedia Adaptation of Iconic Scenes
item.description: A stage adaptation of Irvine Welsh's acclaimed 1993 novel 'Trainspotting' is set to debut in London's West End this summer, three decades after the release of the celebrated film. The story, which fol...

item.title: DA Raises Concerns Over Missing R637 Million in ETDP SETA Funds
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has expressed deep concern following revelations that over R637 million in discretionary grants at the Education, Training and Development Practices Sector Education and T...

item.title: Subway Australasia CEO Shane Bracken shares practical strategies for business growth and leadership
item.description: Shane Bracken, CEO of Subway in Australia and New Zealand, draws on more than 15 years of international experience to explain how businesses can be revived and expanded effectively. Speaking on a lead...

item.title: London Knights begin first-round playoff series against Soo
item.description: The London Knights are set to face the Soo in the opening round of the playoffs, marking the start of an exciting series for fans of Ontario hockey. Both teams are preparing intensively, with the Knig...

item.title: Quebec pilot Antoine Forest dies in Air Canada crash at LaGuardia Airport
item.description: Antoine Forest, a 30-year-old pilot from Coteau-du-Lac, Quebec, has been identified as one of the two pilots killed in an Air Canada flight crash at LaGuardia Airport in New York. The aircraft, en rou...

item.title: Pastor John Anosike and Minister Gayton McKenzie clash over proposed sale of Cape Town’s Good Hope Centre
item.description: A sharp war of words has erupted between Nigerian-born pastor John Anosike and South Africa’s minister of sports, arts and culture, Gayton McKenzie, following the planned sale of Cape Town’s historic ...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Secret Burial of Bodies in Kericho
item.description: The government has been caught off guard after 14 bodies were found buried in one grave at a cemetery in Kericho County, causing national concern. According to the Directorate of Criminal Investigatio...

item.title: Cuba Seeks Tanzanian Media Support to Strengthen Global South Solidarity Amid US Sanctions
item.description: Cuba has urged Tanzanian media organisations to take a stronger role in amplifying the perspectives of the Global South, saying that dominant global narratives often misrepresent developing nations. S...

item.title: Zambales Residents Approve University Charter Updates, Keep PRMSU Name
item.description: PRMSU in Iba, Zambales, is facing challenges with student capacity, staffing, and funding. A proposed House Bill 8520, which aimed to rename President Ramon Magsaysay State University (PRMSU) to Zamba...

item.title: Kwankwaso and Other Political Leaders Hold Talks Toward Possible Alliance Before 2027 Elections
item.description: The Kwankwasiyya Movement don confirm say Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, wey be national leader of the New Nigeria People’s Party (NNPP) and him 2023 presidential candidate, dey in serious discussion with both...

item.title: Four Killed in Two Separate Avalanches in Northwestern British Columbia
item.description: Four people lost their lives in two separate avalanches that struck northwestern British Columbia on Sunday, March 23, 2026. According to the RCMP, three heli-skiers were killed when an avalanche occu...
